hero.body: ProtoGrid helps turn ideas, sketches, broken parts, and custom requirements into prototypes, functional components, and small-batch production.

customParts.title: Custom Parts
customParts.line: Fabricated to spec — one-off or repeat.

whatwedo.heading: What we do
whatwedo.subtext: Four core capabilities. One team from first cut to final delivery.

customParts.title: Custom Parts
customParts.body: You have a design file or a part that needs to exist. We mill, cut, or fabricate it to spec — one-off or repeat.

rapidPrototyping.title: Rapid Prototyping
rapidPrototyping.body: Turn a sketch or concept into a working physical prototype, fast. Test before you commit to tooling or production cost.

partRedesign.title: Part Redesign
partRedesign.body: Broken original, no drawing, outdated component. We reverse-engineer from the physical sample and improve where needed.

smallBatch.title: Small-Batch Production
smallBatch.body: 5 to 200 units with no factory minimums. Consistent quality across the run, delivered as a single shipment.

processSteps.heading: Five steps from task to result

s01.title: Send your task
s01.body: Describe what you need. A sketch, photo, broken part, or a plain text description — whatever you have is a valid starting point.

s02.title: We review
s02.body: We analyze the request, ask clarifying questions if needed, and send back a clear scope and quote within 1 business day.

s03.title: Prototype
s03.body: We build the first version. You receive the actual object — see it, test it, fit it — before committing to a production run.

s04.title: Production
s04.body: Prototype approved. We run the full order — single unit or small batch — with the same setup and quality.

s05.title: Delivery
s05.body: Finished parts shipped or available for local pickup. Documentation included when needed.

r1.title: Physical results, not consultancy
r1.body: We build the thing. Not a report about the thing, not a recommendation. A real object you can hold.

r2.title: No CAD file required
r2.body: A sketch, a broken part, or a written description is enough to start. We figure out the rest.

r3.title: No factory minimums
r3.body: One unit or two hundred — same process, same care, no MOQ pressure.

r4.title: Fast iteration
r4.body: A working prototype in days, not weeks. Catch problems before production, not during it.

r5.title: One point of contact
r5.body: The person who quotes the job is the person who builds it. No handoffs, no translation errors.

r6.title: Transparent scope
r6.body: You get a clear breakdown before we touch anything. The final invoice matches the quote.

faq.heading: Common questions
faq.subtext: Anything not covered here — just ask in the contact form below.

q1.q: Do I need a CAD file to place an order?
q1.a: No. A sketch, photo, or written description is enough to get started. We will ask for what we need after reviewing your request.

q2.q: What is the minimum order quantity?
q2.a: One. We accept single-unit requests as readily as batch orders. There are no minimums.

q3.q: How long does a typical order take?
q3.a: Simple parts: 2–5 working days. Prototypes with design iteration: 1–2 weeks. We give you a clear timeline in the quote, before any work starts.

q4.q: What materials do you work with?
q4.a: Primarily aluminum, steel, and brass for machined and cut parts. If you have specific material requirements — hardness, grade, finish — mention it in your request.

q5.q: Can you work from a broken part with no drawing?
q5.a: Yes. Ship or deliver the physical sample and we will measure, photograph, and reverse-engineer it into a working replacement.

q6.q: What file formats do you accept?
q6.a: .STEP, .DXF, .STL, .IGES, and .F3D are all supported. PDFs with clear dimensions also work. If you have something else, send it anyway — we will let you know if we need a conversion.

q7.a: Currently local and national delivery only. Contact us with your location and we will confirm whether we can cover it.

q8.q: What happens after I submit a request?
q8.a: You get a reply within 1 business day. We will confirm scope, ask any open questions, and send a quote. No work starts until you approve it.

p1.title: Gearbox housing — no drawing available
p1.task: Original housing cracked. No technical drawing existed — only the damaged part.
p1.solution: Reverse-engineered from physical sample, redesigned with 40% thicker walls at the failure point.
p1.result: 6 units produced. All installed and running without issue.

p2.title: Electronics enclosure for dev board
p2.task: Product designer needed a testable housing for a custom PCB before committing to injection moulding.
p2.solution: CNC-machined aluminum enclosure from the provided CAD file. Threaded inserts, removable lid.
p2.result: Prototype delivered in 4 days. Design signed off for tooling.

p3.title: Custom mounting brackets — qty 30
p3.task: 45° angled brackets for a workshop shelving system. Off-the-shelf options didn't fit the load spec.
p3.solution: Plasma-cut 4mm steel, deburred, bent to angle, powder-coated matte black.
p3.result: Full batch in 5 working days. Installed and load-tested by client.
